meta.notes: UK departure airports with terminal, lounge, and facility detail. Destination airport data served via AeroDataBox API in v2+.

LHR.name: London Heathrow Airport

facilities.wifiNotes: Free 45 minutes then paid. Better to use phone hotspot for longer sessions.
facilities.showersNotes: Available in most lounges. Day rooms available at T2 Yotel.
facilities.food: Extensive across all terminals. T5 has the best variety. Most major chains plus independent restaurants.
facilities.hotelNotes: Sofitel in T5, Yotel pods in T2. Several hotels within 5 mins on the Heathrow Express.

transit.interTerminalNotes: T4 and T5 are served by the free Heathrow Express (4 min) and Heathrow Connect. T2/T3 are connected by a walkway. T4↔T2/3/5 requires taking a train — allow at least 20–30 minutes for the transfer alone.
transit.notes: T5 security is often fastest. T2/T3 security can have very long queues — arrive early. If connecting between terminals on a tight layover, alert airport staff.

cityLink.elizabethLine: 35–40 min to central London. Most convenient option. £12–15.
cityLink.heathrowExpress: 15 min to Paddington. Fastest. £25–37 (book online for cheaper).
cityLink.taxi: 30–60 min depending on traffic. Around £50–70 to central London.
cityLink.bus: National Express and other coaches from all terminals. Cheaper but slow.

LHR.tip: Terminal 5 is Heathrow's flagship — cleanest, least crowded, and has the best food. If flying BA, it's worth it. Check your terminal carefully before arriving as they are not connected landside.

LGW.name: London Gatwick Airport

facilities.food: Good range in both terminals. North Terminal generally better variety.
facilities.hotelNotes: Bloc Hotel attached to South Terminal. Several hotels a short shuttle away.

transit.interTerminalNotes: Monorail connects North and South terminals in 3–4 minutes. Runs 24/7.
transit.notes: The monorail is free and quick, but check your terminal carefully. North Terminal security is generally well-managed. South Terminal can get congested in summer.

cityLink.gatwickExpress: 30 min to London Bridge / Victoria. Around £15–20 (book online).
cityLink.thameslink: 30–45 min to London Bridge, Farringdon, St Pancras. Often cheaper than Gatwick Express.
cityLink.bus: easyBus and National Express coaches available. Much cheaper, takes 60–90 min.

LGW.tip: The monorail between terminals runs all night and is very reliable. North Terminal is slightly nicer. Emirates check-in at North Terminal is usually well-organised with a shorter security queue than budget carrier check-ins.

STN.name: London Stansted Airport

facilities.food: Budget airport standard — chains like Costa, Burger King, Wagamama. Nothing exceptional.
facilities.hotelNotes: Radisson Blu and other hotels nearby, free shuttle from terminal.

transit.interTerminalNotes: Single terminal — no inter-terminal transfer needed.
transit.notes: Stansted is compact but can get very crowded during peak budget airline departure times. Security queues can be slow in the morning. Allow extra time.

cityLink.stanstedExpress: 47 min to Liverpool Street. £25–35 (book in advance for best price).
cityLink.bus: National Express and easyBus to various London stops. Much cheaper, takes 75–100 min.

STN.tip: Stansted is a pure budget airport — expect a long walk to gates and basic facilities. If you're flying Ryanair, double-check bag dimensions carefully before you go, as fines are enforced strictly here.

LTN.name: London Luton Airport

facilities.showersNotes: Shower available in the No1 Lounge only.
facilities.food: Limited. Basic chains — Wetherspoon, Costa, Pret. Not much variety.
facilities.hotelNotes: Holiday Inn Luton nearby, free shuttle from terminal.

transit.notes: Luton is the smallest of the London airports. Can feel cramped. The 10-minute shuttle bus to the train station adds time to your journey.

cityLink.trainAndShuttle: 10 min shuttle to Luton Airport Parkway station, then 22–40 min train to St Pancras. Thameslink. Total ~50 min. Around £15–18.
cityLink.bus: National Express, Green Line coaches. Cheaper but slow — 60–90 min.

LTN.tip: The shuttle bus to the train station runs frequently but costs extra. Budget at least 25 minutes from terminal to being seated on the train. Security can be slow — don't cut it fine.

LCY.name: London City Airport

facilities.food: Small but decent selection. Gordon Ramsay Bar & Grill, Travelex, a café.

transit.interTerminalNotes: Single compact terminal — fastest security of any London airport.
transit.notes: London City's biggest advantage is its speed — security queues rarely exceed 10 minutes and check-in closes later than most airports. Perfect for business trips. Limited long-haul options.

cityLink.elizabethLine: Direct from London City Airport station. 10 min to Canary Wharf, 20 min to Liverpool Street, 40 min to Heathrow. Free with Oyster.
cityLink.dlr: DLR also available. Around 20 min to Bank.

LCY.tip: London City is the best airport for a stress-free experience — compact, quiet, and fast. You can check in 45 minutes before departure. The Elizabeth Line makes it very accessible from central London. Limited to short/medium haul (except the New York JFK route via Shannon).

EMA.name: East Midlands Airport

facilities.food: Standard budget airport selection. Wetherspoon, Pret, burger bars.
facilities.hotelNotes: Radisson Blu on-site, connected to terminal.

transit.notes: Compact and manageable. Security can be slow in peak morning hours. Primarily serves the Midlands region.

cityLink.bus: Skylink bus to Leicester (1 hr), Nottingham (1 hr), Derby (45 min), Loughborough (30 min).
cityLink.taxi: 20 min to Nottingham city centre. Around £25. 30 min to Leicester.

EMA.tip: A good option if you live in the Midlands. Parking is cheaper than London airports and less stressful. Primarily short-haul European routes and some winter sun charter flights.

MAN.name: Manchester Airport

facilities.showersNotes: Available in lounges and via the Aspire Day Room.
facilities.food: Good selection in T2, adequate in T1/T3. Multiple restaurants and bars.
facilities.hotelNotes: Radisson Blu, Crowne Plaza, and other hotels at the airport. Connected to terminals.

transit.interTerminalNotes: T1 and T2 are connected by the SkyLink (walkway). T3 requires a bus or a longer walk. Allow at least 15–20 minutes to transfer between T3 and other terminals.
transit.notes: Manchester is the UK's largest regional airport. T2 has been significantly upgraded — best terminal by far for long-haul. Security can be slow, especially in summer. The airport has strict hand luggage rules — check before you go.

cityLink.trainTram: Manchester Airport rail station under T2. 20 min to Manchester Piccadilly, trains to most UK cities. Tram (Metrolink) to Manchester city centre in about 50 min.
cityLink.taxi: 25–40 min to Manchester city centre. Around £25–35.

MAN.tip: Terminal 2 is by far the best — spacious, well-designed, and has the most long-haul flights. If you have a choice, fly from T2. Manchester offers surprisingly good transatlantic and long-haul connections for the North of England.

EDI.name: Edinburgh Airport

facilities.food: Good range — Wetherspoon, Gordon & MacPhail, Caffe Nero, World Duty Free shops.
facilities.hotelNotes: Edinburgh Airport Hotel within the terminal complex.

transit.notes: Generally well-managed airport. Security can be slow during busy Edinburgh periods (Festival in August, Hogmanay at New Year). Tram is excellent for city access.

cityLink.tram: 30 min to Princes Street (city centre). Around £9.50 single. Very reliable.
cityLink.bus: Airlink 100 bus service to city centre. Around £5, takes 25–45 min depending on traffic.

EDI.tip: The tram to the city centre is excellent value and very reliable. During the Edinburgh Festival (August) the airport gets extremely busy — give yourself extra time and book tram tickets in advance.

BHX.name: Birmingham Airport

facilities.food: Decent selection across both terminals. Wetherspoon, Giraffe, Wagamama.
facilities.hotelNotes: Crowne Plaza Birmingham Airport adjacent to terminal.

transit.interTerminalNotes: T1 and T2 are connected by an internal walkway — short walk.
transit.notes: Birmingham is a pleasant, manageable airport. Security is generally efficient. Good for the West Midlands and surrounding areas.

cityLink.train: Air-Rail Link monorail to Birmingham International rail station (1 min, free). Then 10 min to Birmingham New Street by train.
cityLink.taxi: 20–35 min to Birmingham city centre. Around £20–30.

BHX.tip: The Air-Rail Link to Birmingham International makes this the fastest airport to get into from a UK city centre by rail. Excellent for cross-country connections via Birmingham New Street.

BRS.name: Bristol Airport

facilities.food: Standard selection — Costa, Burger King, Caffe Ritazza.
facilities.hotelNotes: Hotel available at the airport. Short taxi to others in the area.

transit.notes: Compact and generally well-run. Security is usually fast. A good alternative to London airports for the South West.

cityLink.bus: Airport Flyer A1 bus to Bristol city centre. Around 30 min, £7–10.
cityLink.taxi: 20–30 min to Bristol city centre. Around £20–30.
cityLink.train: No direct rail link to the terminal — take bus or taxi to Bristol Temple Meads.

BRS.tip: Bristol has no direct rail link, which is its main downside. The bus is reliable but adds time. Parking and drop-off at Bristol are much cheaper than London airports.
